title: Wolf Cola: A Public Relations Nightmare

jokes.text: Is doing cocaine every morning your routine, Frank? - Yeah.
jokes.setup: Character asks if Frank does cocaine every morning as a routine
jokes.punchline: Frank's casual, matter-of-fact confirmation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank character moment - dark, casual admission delivered with zero shame or self-awareness

jokes.text: Well, somebody's chipper this morning. Oh... you guys were there all this time? I didn't see you.
jokes.setup: Frank seems unusually energetic
jokes.punchline: He admits he didn't notice the others because he was high on cocaine
jokes.explanation: Frank's obliviousness to his friends' presence due to drug use is classic IASIP character dysfunction

jokes.text: I'm selling it to the Jews in Boca Raton. They're slurping it up like candy. They're... I'm telling you, they're addicted.
jokes.setup: Frank explains his Wolf Cola business success
jokes.punchline: The casual stereotyping and inappropriate enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Sets up the major confusion that drives the episode - Frank's casual racism and poor business practices

jokes.text: Boca Raton, Frank? Or the official soft drink of Boko Haram?
jokes.setup: Frank claims he's selling to Boca Raton
jokes.punchline: Revelation that he's confused a Florida retirement community with African terrorists
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist premise that drives the entire episode - the confusion between similar-sounding names creates perfect dark comedy

jokes.text: Whoops.
jokes.setup: Frank realizes he's been selling cola to terrorists instead of retirees
jokes.punchline: Completely inadequate, casual response to a massive PR disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery - the complete inadequacy of Frank's response to learning he's been dealing with terrorists is peak Always Sunny

jokes.text: How do you confuse a city in Florida with a terrorist organization in Africa? It's an easy misunderstanding.
jokes.setup: Reasonable question about the mix-up
jokes.punchline: Frank's delusional claim it's an 'easy' mistake
jokes.explanation: Frank's complete lack of self-awareness and ability to rationalize anything

jokes.text: It is if you're an elderly cokehead, trying to launder money through a dummy soda corporation.
jokes.setup: Frank's claim it's an easy mistake
jokes.punchline: Brutally accurate assessment of Frank's situation
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical takedown that perfectly captures Frank's character flaws

jokes.text: My God, there are other fluids?
jokes.setup: Mention of Frank's Fluids expanding beyond Wolf Cola
jokes.punchline: Dee's horrified realization of the scope
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that amplifies the absurdity of Frank's business empire

jokes.text: Fight Milk?! Fight Milk?! Wait, wait, dude, that's our drink.
jokes.setup: List of Frank's fluid products includes Fight Milk
jokes.punchline: Mac and Charlie's shocked recognition of their own product
jokes.explanation: Good callback that connects to established show mythology and escalates the crisis

jokes.text: Remember, we designed that for bodyguards, by bodyguards. And it makes total sense because the-the best bodyguards are former UFC fighters. Well, of course. And the best UFC fighters are former bodyguards, so bodyguards, UFC fighters, that's like... It's a revolving door.
jokes.setup: Explanation of Fight Milk's target market
jokes.punchline: Completely circular and nonsensical logic delivered with complete confidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Mac and Charlie's delusional reasoning - they create a completely circular argument and present it as brilliant logic

jokes.text: Found my go bag. Dee, come over here and dye my hair blond. Go bag? You're gonna go blond?
jokes.setup: Frank prepares to flee from the PR disaster
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of having a 'go bag' and wanting to dye his hair as if this is normal crisis management
jokes.explanation: Frank's paranoid preparation and bizarre escape plan reveal his criminal mindset

jokes.text: Remember when you wanted all the power so you could get the puss? I made you executive vice president of worldwide distribution. Shit, but nobody knows that, right? I mean, dude, I only wanted the illusion of power. The-the illusion. I never saw an ounce of puss.
jokes.setup: Frank reveals Mac has an official title in the company
jokes.punchline: Mac's pathetic admission that his fake power never got him anything
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac character moment - his shallow motivations and complete failure laid bare

jokes.text: You're executive vice president of operations. Oh. What? Yes. Goddamn it, Frank, why would you lump me into this? It's a family business. I wanted it to be wholesome.
jokes.setup: Frank reveals Dee is also implicated
jokes.punchline: Frank's claim he wanted the criminal enterprise to be 'wholesome'
jokes.explanation: Great irony - Frank's idea of 'wholesome' includes money laundering and terrorist sales

jokes.text: 24 hours... This is why we had to wait for you to go to Bed Bath & Beyond?
jokes.setup: Dennis dramatically announcing they have 24 hours to respond
jokes.punchline: Revealing they wasted time on Dee's mundane shopping trip during their crisis
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between the urgent crisis and Dee's prioritizing home goods shopping

jokes.text: This is why we had to wait for you to go to Bed Bath & Beyond? Yes, bitch.
jokes.setup: Dennis arrives late for crisis management
jokes.punchline: His priorities during a PR emergency were shopping at Bed Bath & Beyond
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis moment - narcissistic prioritization of personal needs over crisis

jokes.text: Christ, Mercedes built Nazi tanks, okay? And the Jews are still driving their cars around. Floyd Mayweather beat the shit out of multiple women and he's the highest paid athlete of all time. All right? There's only one thing you can't come back from... banging kids.
jokes.setup: Dennis explains how companies recover from scandals
jokes.punchline: His increasingly inappropriate examples and final declaration
jokes.explanation: Dark, shocking escalation that perfectly captures Dennis's sociopathic business mindset and the show's willingness to go to uncomfortable places

jokes.text: Well, what about the Catholic Church? Hmm. That's a good point. Yeah, no, they did sidestep banging kids pretty masterfully, but they've got billions of dollars at their disposal and we do not, so...
jokes.setup: Dee challenges Dennis's claim about unforgivable acts
jokes.punchline: Dennis's casual analysis of how the Catholic Church 'masterfully' handled abuse scandals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that extends the previous joke with Dennis's sociopathic business analysis

jokes.text: Time to stop eating your crow and start drinking your crow, everybody.
jokes.setup: Mac tries to make a clever marketing pitch
jokes.punchline: Mangled idiom that makes no sense but sounds like it should
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac attempt at being clever that completely misfires

jokes.text: Now, I've always been very passionate about dominating other men. Uh-oh. There's nothing like the feeling of another man submitting to your will. Now that's power. Yeah. In a lot of ways, that's love. What are you talking about? Okay, he's a bodyguard, though.
jokes.setup: Mac tries to pitch Fight Milk to UFC guys
jokes.punchline: His accidentally homoerotic description of domination
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac cringe moment - his repressed sexuality leaking into a business pitch, creating maximum awkwardness

jokes.text: We're-we're a couple of crow enthusiasts who wanted to take the power and essence of a crow and-and we packed that into a delicious milk drink that we invented, thus Fight Milk.
jokes.setup: Charlie tries to explain Fight Milk logically
jokes.punchline: The completely insane premise presented as if it makes perfect sense
jokes.explanation: Pure Charlie logic - completely insane premise delivered with absolute confidence and sincerity

jokes.text: Yeah, man, I'd get up and shake your hands if I wasn't shitting my brains out right now. Oh, no. No, no, that's fine, yeah.
jokes.setup: Mac and Charlie meet their UFC hero
jokes.punchline: He's violently ill from their product
jokes.explanation: Great reversal - their moment of triumph becomes immediate humiliation

jokes.text: Makes you expel all body fluids. Sweat, piss, and shit; we got these weigh-ins coming up. Nothing cuts weight like Fight Milk.
jokes.setup: Explanation of why fighters use Fight Milk
jokes.punchline: It's not an energy drink, it's a laxative
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - their energy drink is actually making people violently sick, but it's useful for the wrong reasons

jokes.text: Oh, I just puked on my dick! Rock and roll.
jokes.setup: Fighter is violently ill from Fight Milk
jokes.punchline: Graphic description delivered with inexplicable enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that works through sheer shock value and the fighter's bizarre positive attitude

jokes.text: You want to change... the perception of a global terrorist organization? Yeah, and I've got many facts to do it.
jokes.setup: Frank's PR strategy is revealed
jokes.punchline: He wants to improve Boko Haram's image with 'facts'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist premise - Frank's solution to being associated with terrorists is to make terrorists more likeable

jokes.text: You see, audiences are gonna trust me over you because you're a woman, and, by definition, shrill. No. Women identify with women in power. Oh, no, Dee. Women hate other women in power. I'm threatened by 'em. Everyone is.
jokes.setup: Dennis explains his PR strategy
jokes.punchline: Casual sexism presented as marketing wisdom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis sociopathy - treating misogyny as a business strategy with complete confidence

jokes.text: Now, objectify yourself and humanize me.
jokes.setup: Dennis gives Dee her role in the PR appearance
jokes.punchline: Brazenly stating his manipulative strategy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis line - maximum sociopathy delivered with complete directness and economy

jokes.text: Women identify with women in power. Oh, no, Dee. Women hate other women in power. I'm threatened by 'em. Everyone is.
jokes.setup: Dee arguing she should speak on TV because women relate to her
jokes.punchline: Dennis's cynical but arguably accurate take on female competition
jokes.explanation: Dennis's manipulative psychology mixed with uncomfortable social observations

jokes.text: And also, we're sorry. We accept full responsibility for this heinous mistake, and please know that I am actively firing people as we speak, because I am the boss. Bitch.
jokes.setup: Dee goes off-script during the TV interview
jokes.punchline: Her terrible ad-libbing that makes everything worse, ending with calling the host 'bitch'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation where Dee's desperation to assert authority completely backfires

jokes.text: You know what, Chet, can I say something? I think we've said enough. I think... you got to understand that Boko Haram did not do 9/11. Yeah. That was... al-Qaeda. Paris, that was done by ISIS. Boko Haram is just concentrating, they're doing Africa. I mean... I mean, there's mosquitos down there that are bigger terrorists than Boko Haram.
jokes.setup: Frank decides to defend Boko Haram on live TV
jokes.punchline: His bizarre terrorism comparison and mosquito analogy
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant escalation - Frank's attempt at damage control by defending terrorists on live TV, with the mosquito comparison being peak absurdist comedy

jokes.text: Boko Haram did not do 9/11. That was... al-Qaeda. Paris, that was done by ISIS. Boko Haram is just concentrating, they're doing Africa.
jokes.setup: Frank trying to defend his association with terrorists
jokes.punchline: Casually discussing terrorist organizations like competing brands with market segments
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist take treating terrorist organizations like business competitors with territories

jokes.text: People calling me a bitch, flat-chested bitch, pretty much everybody wanting to rape me.
jokes.setup: Dee reads online reactions to her TV appearance
jokes.punchline: The increasingly violent and personal nature of the attacks
jokes.explanation: Dark commentary on online harassment culture, though the execution is somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Now social media will come down on you with the fury of a middle-aged man who's accomplished nothing.
jokes.setup: Dennis explains social media dynamics
jokes.punchline: Specific and accurate description of online trolls
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary delivered with Dennis's characteristic pseudo-intellectual confidence

jokes.text: Are you fat? You probably are, 'cause you're watching TV! Or Internet.
jokes.setup: Mac's Fight Milk commercial
jokes.punchline: Immediately insulting the potential customers
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac terrible marketing instinct - attacking the audience while trying to sell them something

jokes.text: It's easy for us men... we have four acceptable body types. Skinny ripped, jack ripped, dad bod, and fat... if you're funny. It's a landslide! As we all know, women only have one acceptable body type. Skinny, big tits!
jokes.setup: Mac's analysis of body standards
jokes.punchline: Crude but accurate social observation delivered with complete confidence
jokes.explanation: Mac's typical blend of crude observation and surprising social awareness

jokes.text: Aah! No nerds! Fight Milk!
jokes.setup: Charlie appears to attack someone in the commercial
jokes.punchline: Random violence against 'nerds' as marketing strategy
jokes.explanation: Pure Charlie chaos - unprovoked violence as product promotion

jokes.text: Really, I mean, between the three of you, we could be opening up a bakery with all the muffin tops I'm seeing.
jokes.setup: Mac tries to sell Fight Milk to ring girls
jokes.punchline: Insulting potential customers' bodies while trying to sell them a product
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac terrible sales technique - body shaming as marketing strategy

jokes.text: No! We're not the diarrhea guys. - No! - That's not what I want to be.
jokes.setup: Being called the diarrhea drink makers
jokes.punchline: Desperate denial of their obvious brand identity
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing their delusion about their brand

jokes.text: We're not into your weird diarrhea drink.
jokes.setup: Mac's terrible sales pitch to the ring girls
jokes.punchline: Blunt, accurate assessment of Fight Milk
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response that cuts through Mac's delusions

jokes.text: It's not for women! It was never made for women! But it'd be great if women also drank it. The dream was to create a muscle-up energy drink that gave you the power to fight like a crow! Yes! Men! Or women. But yes. But definitely crow-power fighting skills.
jokes.setup: Charlie and Mac defend their product vision
jokes.punchline: Increasingly contradictory and insane justifications
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Mac and Charlie's desperate backpedaling and crow obsession

jokes.text: Well, if you're asking me if I like wild animals slobbering all over me, then, yeah, I guess I'm not really a... not really a dog lover then.
jokes.setup: Host asks if Dennis likes dogs during interview
jokes.punchline: Dennis's weirdly clinical and off-putting response
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis sociopathy - making liking dogs sound creepy and unnatural

jokes.text: I should be able to eat me some fried dog. Know what I'm saying? I should be able to eat a bucket of fried dog at a chicken fight.
jokes.setup: Dennis argues for consistency in animal treatment
jokes.punchline: Advocating for eating dogs and attending chicken fights
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation where Dennis's logical argument leads to increasingly disturbing conclusions

jokes.text: 'cause that's just the crow eggs and some of the crow's fecal matter.
jokes.setup: Charlie explains the ingredients of Fight Milk Classic
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning crow feces as an ingredient
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - casually revealing horrible product ingredients as if it's normal

jokes.text: What the hell is going on? Who are these guys? Uh, just like to take the opportunity to thank Fight Milk. Yes, and thank you, Cowboy, and thank you, Megan. We'd also like to take this opportunity to announce that Fight Milk is now the official drink of the UFC. Official drink. No, no. This is not the official drink of the UFC. And I don't know who these guys are. Security, get these assholes out of here!
jokes.setup: Mac and Charlie crash the UFC weigh-in
jokes.punchline: Their delusional announcement immediately shut down by officials
jokes.explanation: Good cringe escalation where their moment of triumph is immediately destroyed

jokes.text: Turns out there are two things that can't be forgiven... raping children and disliking dogs.
jokes.setup: Dennis reflects on the PR disaster
jokes.punchline: Updating his earlier theory with the dog incident
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that updates Dennis's sociopathic business theory with new information

jokes.text: We pumped it full of human growth hormone, you know. Yeah. I mean, just nothing else was working. It just... it was just giving people diarrhea.
jokes.setup: Mac and Charlie explain why fighters tested positive
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of illegal drug use as problem-solving
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing their criminal negligence presented as logical business decision

jokes.text: If you give it enough time, something new will come along to replace peoples' old outrage with new outrage.
jokes.setup: Dennis explains the 24-hour news cycle
jokes.punchline: Accurate social commentary about modern media
jokes.explanation: Sharp media observation, though somewhat predictable for Dennis

jokes.text: Goddamn it!
jokes.setup: News reveals Fight Milk is also connected to Frank's Fluids
jokes.punchline: Frank's realization that he can't escape the cycle
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective ending beat that closes the circular disaster

jokes.text: Start the clock. Yeah.
jokes.setup: The 24-hour news cycle begins again
jokes.punchline: Frank accepting they're back to square one
jokes.explanation: Good circular ending that ties back to the episode's central theme
